articles.title: AI X COMMERCE
articles.summary: Google's risk isn't losing all search, but losing high-value commerce queries to AI. Amazon and Shopify are better positioned, but true AI-driven buying requires cleaner data, unified APIs, user memory, and embedded feedback.

articles.title: 5 MARKETING TRENDS I CAN'T STOP THINKING ABOUT
articles.summary: Master existing tools, use constraints to fuel creativity, embrace polarizing ideas for attention, sound human to stand out, and find unconventional platforms where your audience already gathers.

articles.title: WHEN YOUR PRODUCT'S PIPELINE STALLS AND THE RUNWAY IS SHORT
articles.summary: When pipelines stall, product survival depends on shifting from reactive sales support to proactive partnership. PMs can revive growth by uncovering sales challenges, proving ROI, aligning on outcomes, and building trust - earning both time and traction.

articles.title: WHY DESIGN DEBT DESTROYS REPUTATIONS (AND HOW TO FIX IT)
articles.summary: Inheriting design debt can quietly damage both product performance and a PM's reputation. Treat it like technical debt - audit flows, measure impact, reframe fixes as value gains, and integrate cleanup into feature work to protect user experience and career credibility.

articles.title: HOW TO USE DEEP RESEARCH FOR GTM
articles.summary: Deep Research tools can slash GTM research from hours to minutes when guided with clear goals, context, and source preferences. This playbook compares top tools, shares prompting tips, and outlines five practical GTM applications, from project planning to market expansion.

articles.title: PRD EXAMPLE: BEST PRACTICE REQUIREMENTS IN ACTION 👀
articles.summary: A strong PRD links directly to strategy, defines measurable success, incorporates real customer feedback, and leaves space for decisions and unknowns. Done well, it becomes a live, collaborative guide for delivering value - clear enough to align teams, lean enough to avoid bloat.

articles.title: QUESTIONING MY BEST INSTINCTS → MORE LEARNING
articles.summary: Over-relying on risk-spotting can stifle experimentation. Questioning biases and inviting your team's input builds more balanced, open leadership.

articles.title: THREE PERSPECTIVES
articles.summary: The CEO, PM, and VP each see different truths - urgency, operational strain, and trust gaps - but share goals of innovation, focus, and efficiency. Blending bold action, structure, and empathy can turn tension into sustainable progress.

articles.summary: Google just made it possible for anyone to build a free, no-code AI assistant in under five minutes.

articles.title: THE FIRST MINUTE TEST
articles.summary: The First Minute Test asks if users gain certainty, trust, and curiosity in 60 seconds - because if they don't, they won't return.
